title: A Trip Around the Moon: What's Next?
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission was a big success! Four astronauts flew around the Moon and came back safely in the Orion spacecraft. They took amazing pictures that made many kids excited about space. But going to the Moon is just the first step. The real challenge is to build a place where people can live and work on the Moon, like a Moon base. NASA plans to land astronauts on the Moon every year starting in 2028. However, building the special landers needed for this is taking longer than expected. Companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin are working on these landers, but they are behind schedule. So, while the mission was a triumph, there is still a lot of hard work ahead to make living on the Moon possible.

keywords.explanation: the space agency in the United States that explores space
the big rock that orbits Earth and we see at night
special spacecraft that can land on the Moon
a plan that says when things should happen

How many astronauts were on the Artemis II mission?
What spacecraft did the astronauts use?
What is NASA's plan for the Moon starting in 2028?
Why is it hard to build a Moon base?
Which companies are building the landers?

background_read: Space exploration is about sending people and machines to learn about places like the Moon and Mars. NASA is a group that does this for the United States. Astronauts are brave people who go on these trips. The Moon is Earth's closest neighbor in space, and scientists want to study it and maybe live there someday. Building things in space is tricky because it takes a lot of time and special equipment.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Sent astronauts around the Moon and plan to build a Moon base
WHY: To explore space and maybe let people live on the Moon

perspectives.description: The mission was a success and shows we can explore space more.
There are big challenges ahead, like building landers on time.
